claims_verified.claim: AC2 budget: real auth-error message stays well under 300
AC2 worst case with max-length snippet also fits
AgentResult.text is non-optional str, no None-handling gap
No other code depends on the exact pre-change error string
claims_verified.evidence: grep for 'not trustworthy'/'did not complete cleanly' across orchestrator/ and tests/ finds only verdict.py and the updated test_verdict.py assertions

claims_verified.claim: Other request_payload callers (investigator/explorer in loop.py) unaffected
claims_verified.evidence: loop.py:960,994 call request_payload generically; existing tests using error AgentResult fixtures (test_run_cli.py:447, test_loop_report_only.py:405,428) don't assert on message content excluding the agent text, gate is green
ruff 'All checks passed!'; basedpyright '0 errors, 1558 warnings'; pytest '707 passed'

findings.summary: Comment references a 196-char figure that doesn't match the chosen _ERROR_TEXT_MAX=180 three lines below, likely stale from an earlier draft.

test_assessment: Every AC has a directly-mapped, passing test (test_verdict.py + test_local_merge.py new cases), and the existing fail-closed / quota tests remain green unmodified, confirming no regression in the shared request_payload core.
residual_risks: AC2's byte budget is only implicitly coupled across two modules (verdict.py's _ERROR_TEXT_MAX and local_merge.py's _ABSTENTION_REASON_MAX) with no shared assertion; a future bump to max_retries or a longer base prefix could silently reintroduce double-truncation.
